title: An Emboldened Israel Is Seizing Opportunities to Remake Region
summary: With the war against Iran underway, and the U.S. military as a powerful ally, the Israeli government is seizing its chance to move against other adversaries.

title: Global Markets Tumble as Iran War Intensifies
summary: Stocks and bonds slumped as investors retrenched from the uncertain and unpredictable effects that the fighting could have on the world economy.

title: ‘Pray We Make It Through the Night’: Iran’s Capital Under Siege
summary: Many residents of Tehran managed to get out of town when the U.S. and Israel attack began, but others who could not described living under bombardment.

title: Iran’s Escalating Retaliation, and Takeaways From the Clintons’ Epstein Testimony
summary: Plus, fighting phone scams.

title: Texas and North Carolina Elections Will Test Older House Democrats’ Staying Power
summary: Starting in North Carolina on Tuesday, older House Democrats face a test of their staying power and the appetite of voters to replace them.

title: Crockett vs. Talarico. Progressive vs. Moderate. Right?
summary: The Texas Democratic Senate primary is more about persona than policy, and the same is true for many other races.

title: Austin Shooting Puts Islam at Center of Tuesday’s Primaries in Texas
summary: Politicians quickly cited the attack in their campaign messaging. Some Republicans called for stopping immigration, while Democrats called for gun control.

title: Ford and G.M. Face a Dilemma as China Excels in Electric Vehicles
summary: General Motors, Ford and other established automakers risk becoming relics if they don’t catch up to Chinese carmakers and technology companies in electric vehicles and self-driving cars.

title: Tariffs Confound Small Businesses Again
summary: Chocolate makers and fig-paste importers are facing a tangle of unknowns, including whether to seek refunds for tariffs invalidated by the Supreme Court.

title: Veterans Affairs Department Cut Thousands of Roles for Doctors and Nurses
summary: The Trump administration has eliminated health care positions left vacant after a wave of resignations and retirements at the V.A. last year.

title: Trump Administration Abandons Efforts to Impose Executive Orders on Law Firms
summary: The move amounts to a surrender in a clash that has led many law firms to submit to the president rather than face the threat of his executive orders.

title: Noem to Address Congress for First Time Since Minnesota Immigration Surge
summary: Kristi Noem, the homeland security secretary, is also likely to face questions about how her department’s shutdown is affecting counterterrorism work after the U.S. assault on Iran.

title: OpenAI Amends A.I. Deal With the Pentagon
summary: The new pact includes additional protections to prevent the use of the company’s technology for mass surveillance of Americans.

title: Seven Takeaways From the Clintons’ Epstein Depositions
summary: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton was defiant. Former President Bill Clinton spoke of President Trump’s ties to Epstein. A Republican raised a conspiracy theory.

title: In South Carolina Measles Outbreak, Vaccine Skepticism Led to Largest Outbreak Since 2000
summary: Spartanburg County in South Carolina is ground zero for the largest measles outbreak since 2000. One school has a vaccination rate of 21 percent.

title: As Trump Scrambles the World Order, Can Germany Learn the Language of Hard Power?
summary: In the wake of the U.S. bombing of Iran and its dismissal of European allies, an anxious continent’s best chance at security runs through its largest economy.

title: When Trump Meets Germany’s Merz, Iran Will Likely Dominate Discussion
summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s visit to Washington, long planned, is now likely to be dominated by discussions of the attack, which Europeans did not take part in.

title: Germany Has Ratcheted Up Defense Spending. In Europe, That Worries Some People.
summary: Soon the country’s armed forces budget could exceed those of Britain and France combined. In Paris, there are concerns that European “strategic autonomy” will have a German accent.

title: Colleges See Spike in Students With Disabilities, Including Elite Schools
summary: Universities from Harvard to Hampshire have admitted significantly more students with disabilities over the last decade, as diagnoses for A.D.H.D. and anxiety increase.

title: In Myanmar, Junta Leader Eyes the Title of President
summary: The military ruler of Myanmar, Senior Gen. Min Aung Hlaing, is expected to seek a civilian post to add a veneer of legitimacy after sham elections.

title: Mamdani’s Parks Dept. Aims to Offer More Recreation With Smaller Budget
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s Parks Department plans to become New York City’s “agency of affordability,” though the mayor has not increased the agency’s funding.

title: Trump’s Foreign Policy Vision: Imperialism
summary: Donald Trump’s foreign policy vision is imperialism

title: Nine Law Firms Surrendered. Four Law Firms Won.
summary: The four law firms that fought Trump’s executive orders have forced him to retreat.

title: A Danish Program Takes On the Stigma of Mental Illness
summary: One of Us, run by Denmark’s health ministry, works with people with mental health conditions to share their stories in schools, hospitals and police stations, helping turn fear into understanding.

title: Pizza Hut Take Fans on a Trip to the Past
summary: With heaps of nostalgia but little promotion by their parent chain, Pizza Hut Classics take their fervent fans on a time trip back to a simpler, warmer era.

title: Under Pressure from Trump, Cuban Leader Calls for ‘Urgent’ Economic Change
summary: President Miguel Díaz-Canel said the private sector needed more autonomy, as the island confronts a U.S. oil blockade that has deepened a humanitarian crisis.

title: U.S. Slaps Sanctions on Rwanda, Saying It Sabotaged Trump Peace Deal
summary: Rwanda’s government responded by claiming the sanctions unjustly targeted only one party to the conflict and misrepresented the facts.
